title: Pre-paid-traffic resilience for official-source connectors
objective: Add connector-layer resilience before paid traffic: response caching, Retry-After honoring, jittered backoff, circuit breaker, last-known-good serving with visible staleness labeling, and per-analysis request budgets
inputs: Owner code-audit directive 2026-07-17 pre-paid-traffic resilience items (verbatim in project-control/reports/OWNER-REVIEW-code-audit-task-consolidation.md section 2.5)
services/api SODA client + fixture transport seam (M1-T002/M1-T005 connector framework)
M1-T003/M1-T004 research: documented Socrata rate limits, tokenless throttling behavior, Retry-After semantics
M2-T003 outputs (staleness labeling rides on the validated response contract; last-known-good payloads must still pass backend validation)
outputs: Connector-layer resilience in services/api: response caching with TTL + versioned keys; Retry-After honored exactly; jittered exponential backoff; per-source circuit breaker (open/half-open/closed); last-known-good serving with staleness surfaced in provenance and coverage (never silent); per-analysis request budget with typed budget-exceeded failure
Deterministic tests for every behavior against the fixture transport (no live calls in CI)
Metrics/log hooks for breaker state, cache hit ratio, budget consumption (observability-ready, no secrets in logs)
forbidden_paths: packages/contracts/** (staleness fields, if contract-visible, go through an M2-T003-pipeline additive change reviewed at G1)
acceptance_scenarios: S1 cache: second identical request within TTL serves from cache (transport call-count proves no upstream hit); expiry refetches
S2 Retry-After: 429 with Retry-After delays exactly as instructed - no early retry (clock-controlled test)
S3 backoff: retries use bounded exponential backoff with jitter (distribution asserted over runs); max-retry cap respected
S4 breaker: opens after the configured failure threshold, rejects fast while open, half-opens after cooldown, closes on success - all state transitions tested
S5 last-known-good: upstream down -> LKG served WITH visible staleness (retrieved_at + stale flag in provenance/coverage); never silently presented as fresh; no LKG available -> typed failure
S6 budget: per-analysis request budget exceeded -> typed budget_exceeded failure, zero further upstream calls in that analysis
S7 idempotency: retried requests are idempotent; no duplicate side effects; provenance records actual retrieval, not retry count artifacts
S8 regression: full existing connector/API suite green; no live-network dependence added to CI
risks: Staleness must be visible at the contract level - coordinate any contract-visible field as an additive M2-T003-pipeline change
Clock-dependent tests must be deterministic (injected clock, no sleeps in CI)

progress_log.message: Producer implementation complete (resilience layer: cache/Retry-After/backoff/breaker/LKG-visible-staleness/budget; 264 tests green); PR 20 merged to main at 9100210 after full CI green incl. fixed control-plane
